**Updating config file**

I'm updating only `static/admin/config.yml` to add a `slug` field with widget `string`. I'm adding a `slug` field with widget `string` to `static/admin/config.yml`. 

I'm changing the `categories` field from a single string to a list widget in the admin config. I'm updating the config to use list widget for tags field with string items. I'm adjusting the list widget config to use primitive mode for string items in Netlify CMS. I'm correcting the Netlify CMS config to use the proper list widget structure for string arrays in Hugo. I'm switching Decap's list widget to use string-only format for Hugo compatibility. I'm updating the Decap list widget config to use string-only format by removing the inner field's name. 

I'm configuring the Decap list widget with string-only format by removing the inner field's name and using simple string syntax. I'm adding an explicit slug field to avoid conflicts with Hugo's slugification and ensuring Sveltia CMS schema compatibility. I'm marking the slug field as optional with a hint, since the filename generation depends on it being populated. 

I'm adding an optional slug field with a hint and a date-time widget for proper Hugo date formatting. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Checking slug behavior**

I'm investigating how Decap handles empty slugs for new posts - if `slug: "{{slug}}"` resolves to empty, it may break entry creation. I found that empty slugs from the slug field can result in broken or empty filenames, so I'll implement validation to prevent this. 

**Configuring slug templates**

I'll use `slug: "{{year}}-{{month}}-{{day}}-{{slug}}"` to ensure consistent filename generation while preserving custom slug overrides. I'll derive collection slugs from title when slug field is empty, using `slug: "{{year}}-{{month}}-{{slug}}"` pattern. 

I'll switch to `identifier_field: title` with a safe `slug` template to avoid empty slugs. I'll remove filename template's dependence on optional slug field to prevent empty string overwrite issues. I'll use `slug: "{{slug}}"` and document that the slug field should match the expected format, otherwise default decap slug behavior will be used. I'm clarifying the distinction between file slug (from filename) and frontmatter slug (from field) to avoid confusion in slug generation logic. I clarified that `{{slug}}` in templates refers to the filename slug, not the frontmatter `slug` field. 

I confirmed that the frontmatter `slug` field is independent of `{{slug}}` in filename templates, which is auto-generated from the title. I confirmed that frontmatter `slug` field is independent of `{{slug}}` in filename templates, with Hugo respecting params.slug for permalink. 

**Checking slug consistency**

I found inconsistency between filenames and frontmatter slugs for some posts, particularly 'power-of-worship' where the filename and slug differ. I'm verifying YAML validity and fixing multiline object formatting for linter compliance. </details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Here’s what changed.

### Updates in `static/admin/config.yml`

Blog posts in `content/english/blog` already use more frontmatter than the CMS exposed. The collection is aligned with that pattern (see e.g. `blog-template.md` and `how-i-built-joraps-world.md`):

| Field | Widget | Notes |
|--------|--------|--------|
| `slug` | string | Optional; matches your custom permalink when set |
| `meta_title` | string | Optional SEO title |
| `author` | string | Default `JoRap` |
| `image` | image | Uses `static/images` → `/images` |
| `categories` | list of strings | Inner field has no `name` so Hugo gets a real string array |
| `tags` | list of strings | Same |
| `draft` | boolean | Default `false` |

`title`, `description`, `date`, and `body` are unchanged.

`public/` is in `.gitignore`, so the file you had open under `public/admin/` is from a local `hugo` build. The source of truth is **`static/admin/config.yml`**; after a build or `hugo server`, `public/admin/config.yml` will match again.

---

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Checking asset paths**

I'm investigating how Hugo handles asset paths with double slashes after concatenating 'assets/' and image paths. I found that string concatenation of 'assets/' + '/images/alex-eala.jpg' creates a double slash path, but fileExists might still work on Unix. 

I confirmed that Go's `path/filepath` package normalizes double slashes, so `assets//images/alex-eala.jpg` resolves correctly to `assets/images/alex-eala.jpg`. I confirmed that Hugo's `resources.Get` expects paths relative to the assets directory, not absolute paths starting with `/`. I found that fileExists is checking the wrong path due to incorrect string concatenation in Hugo templating. 

I confirmed Hugo accepts paths with or without leading slashes, and identified that the CMS media_folder should be changed to `assets/images`. 

**Updating CMS config**

I'm updating the CMS media_folder to `assets/images` to match Hugo's expected structure. I'm fixing the path construction issue where `assets/` + `/images/foo.jpg` creates `assets//images/foo.jpg` due to double slashes. I'm setting media_folder to 'assets/images' and public_folder to '/images' to fix the media library issue. </details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Images live in `assets/images/` (e.g. `alex-eala.jpg`, `joraps-world.jpg`). CMS was set to `static/images`, which only contains the admin UI — so the media library was empty. Pointing `media_folder` to `assets/images` and keeping `public_folder: "/images"` so frontmatter stays `/images/...` and matches the theme's `assets/` + path checks.



_**Agent (auto-low)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2">

</details>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/static/admin/config.yml</summary>

</details>

</tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
Sveltia was using **`media_folder: "static/images"`**. Your real image files are under **`assets/images/`** (e.g. `alex-eala.jpg`, `joraps-world.jpg`). `static/images` is effectively empty aside from the admin UI, so the CMS had nothing to list.

### Change
In `static/admin/config.yml`, **`media_folder` is now `assets/images`**.

**`public_folder` stays `/images`**, so new uploads still get paths like `/images/filename.jpg`, which matches your existing frontmatter and the theme’s logic (`assets/` + that path for `fileExists` / `resources.Get` in `themes/jorap/layouts/partials/components/image.html`).

After deploy or a hard refresh of `/admin/`, the media library should show everything under `assets/images/`. New uploads from the CMS will land in that folder as well.

---
